claim_summary: Galaxies form within larger dark matter halos, starting as gas which cools inside halos and forms stars

Low mass dwarf galaxies generally have dark matter halos >100× more massive than their stellar mass; low mass dwarf galaxies typically have dark matter halos two orders of magnitude greater than stellar mass
Discovery of NGC 1052-DF2, first galaxy lacking dark matter with total mass consistent with stellar mass alone
Discovery of NGC 1052-DF4, second galaxy lacking dark matter with total mass consistent with stellar mass alone; DF4 has similar age and metallicity to DF9; assumption of ±0.5 uncertainty in M_★/L
DF2 total mass consistent with stellar mass
DF2 total mass measurement; DF2 velocity dispersion measurement

DF2 and DF4 both have extremely luminous globular clusters
DF2 and DF4 are ultra-diffuse galaxies
Discovery of tight linear alignment of dozen faint galaxies in NGC 1052 field including DF2 and DF4
Wider diffuse object catalog used for trail uniqueness analysis
Wider diffuse object catalog used for trail uniqueness analysis

Trail galaxies are kinematically connected with velocities linearly increasing based on position along trail; DF9 distance estimate of 20.6 Mpc based on trail position; measured velocities of two star clusters in DF9 from Keck/LRIS spectra; in-depth review of potential formation theories and likelihood for observed galaxy trail; linear trail distance relation assumption
Simulations of bullet dwarf scenario showing similar alignment of galaxies; bullet dwarf simulations predicted trail before discovery
Simulations of bullet dwarf scenario, explains luminous globular clusters
Proposal of bullet dwarf galaxy formation scenario where dark matter-free galaxies arise from gas separated from dark matter by high-speed collision
Bullet Cluster - galaxy cluster scale analog
Bullet dwarf simulations explain DF2/DF4 large sizes
Bullet dwarf explains nearly monochromatic globular clusters

Follow-up bullet dwarf simulations confirm observed trail properties consistent with single high-speed collision; simulation of bullet dwarf scenario specific to NGC 1052 system, demonstrates survivability under tidal field; modest scatter in linear distance expectation from bullet dwarf simulations

Estimates binary orbital motion effect as σ_binaries = α × σ_b
Dense systems like globular clusters have α~10% binary fraction due to dynamical interactions
Low stellar density systems in Ursa Minor have up to α~100% binary fraction with strong metallicity dependence
Recent revision of star cluster UNIONS I dispersion from 3.7 to 0.1 km/s largely due to single binary
Formula for calculating mass within effective half-light radius M_e = 3σ²r_e,3D/G
